privileged_info_summary: This is a synthetic Java/Spring Boot application task using Jakarta Persistence JPA. The project is Spring Boot 3.1.5 targeting Java 17 with H2 in-memory database. The schema defines two tables: 'category' (id, name) and 'product' (product_id, sku_code, unit_price, category_id FK). The Category entity is missing @Entity annotation. The Product entity has incorrect field-to-column mappings: 'id' should map to 'product_id', 'sku' should map to 'sku_code', 'price' should map to 'unit_price', and 'categoryId' (Long) should be refactored to a 'Category category' field with @ManyToOne relationship and @JoinColumn(name='category_id'). The MappingIntegrationTest verifies fixes by saving entities and querying the database to confirm column names and foreign key relationships. The verifier checks: (1) @Entity annotation on Category, (2) correct @Column name mappings on Product, (3) @ManyToOne relationship with Category type field, and (4) that schema.sql was not modified.
